chunks.text: Order Sheet IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH KARACHI CP No.S-91 of 2026 ____________________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature(s) of Judge(s) 1. For orders on office objection Nos.1 to 3 a/w reply as at A 2. For hearing of CMA No.567/2026 3. Hearing of main case 09.02.2026 Mr. Javaid Ahmed Rajput, advocate for the petitioner --------- The case of the petitioner is that Family Suit No.767 of 2021 filed by the respondent Mst. Zoya Anjum was decreed by the learned Family Judge Karachi South, wherein the petitioner filed application for depositing the decretal amount in monthly installments of Rs. 15000/- per month. Such application was declined by the executing Court vide order dated 19.05.2025 and the learned appellate Court vide order dated 24.12.2025 on the ground that no appeal lies against the interim orders. 2. Counsel for the petitioner submits that financial capacity of the petitioner allows him to pay decretal amount in installments of Rs. 15000/- per month which he volunteers but the Courts below declined such request. He, therefore, prayed that request of the petitioner for payment of decretal amount in installments may be acceded to. 3. It appears that the learned trial Court decreed the Suit of the respondent for an amount of Rs. 20,000/- per month from March 2021 till her Iddat period and Rs. 474,000/- in respect of dowry articles. Out of the said amount, the petitioner has already deposited Rs. 200,000/-. The petitioner has not refused to deposit the amount but he only needs installments to facilitate for the payment of decretal amount which request ought to have been considered by the learned Family Court keeping in view the financial status of the petitioner. 4.

Out of the said amount, the petitioner has already deposited Rs. 200,000/-. The petitioner has not refused to deposit the amount but he only needs installments to facilitate for the payment of decretal amount which request ought to have been considered by the learned Family Court keeping in view the financial status of the petitioner. 4. Since the petitioner has made a request for making monthly installments of the payment of decretal amount, therefore, this petition is granted. The orders dated 19.05.2025 & 24.12.2025 passed by the P a g e | 2 Courts below in Execution Application No.52/2022 (re-Mst. Zoha Anjum v. Obaid Talha) are set aside. The petitioner shall deposit an amount of Rs. 20,000/- per month till satisfaction of the decree. 5. This petition stands disposed of in the above terms alongwith pending application(s). Office to send a copy of this order to the learned trial Court. JUDGE Nadir*
